Text1: When an evidence item of an assurance project is related to other evidence items of the project by means of evidence traceability links, the OPENCOSS platform shall inform the user that the evaluation of the evidence item might depend on the evaluation of the others.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall show the actions required in an assurance project as a result of evidence item changes.

Text1: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the choice to drop evidence traceability links of an assurance project.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the ability to drop evidence traceability links of an assurance project.

Text1: When a user has indicated a possible change in an evidence item of an assurance project, the OPENCOSS platform shall indicate the evidence items of the assurance project that would be affected by the change.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the ability to indicate if an action required in an assurance project as a result of the change of an evidence item has been addressed.

Text1: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the ability to indicate whether the specification of characteristics for an evidence item of an assurance project is regarded as complete.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall be able to generate reports about the body of evidence of an assurance project by means of documents in PDF, RTF, and MS Word formats.

Text1: The OPENCOSS platform shall be able to automatically associate an artefact in an external tool to an evidence item of an assurance project imported from the external tool.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform must be able to automatically indicate the tool that stores the artefact associated to an evidence item of an assurance project imported from external tool.

Text1: The OPENCOSS platform shall be able to generate reports about the body of evidence of an assurance project in the form of HTML files.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the ability to define tables to show traceability between the evidence items of an assurance project.

Text1: When the status of an action required in an assurance project as a result of evidence evaluation is "pending", the OPENCOSS platform shall colour such an action in red.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the ability to indicate the evidence type of the current evidence items of an assurance project.

Text1: The OPENCOSS platform must provide users with the ability to modify the intermediate nodes in the tree-view for display of the evidence items of an assurance project only once.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform shall provide users with the ability to modify the intermediate nodes in the tree-view for display of the evidence items of an assurance project.

Text1: The OPENCOSS platform shall be able to export evidence traceability links of an assurance project to external tools.
Text2: The OPENCOSS platform must be able to send out evidence traceability links of an assurance project to external and internal tools.

Text1: When an evidence traceability link of an assurance project contains information imported from an external tool, and the information in the OPENCOSS platform is modified, the OPENCOSS platform shall indicate such a modification by colouring the evidence traceability link in red.
Text2: When an evidence traceability link of an assurance project contains information imported from an external tool, and the information in the external tool is modified, the OPENCOSS platform shall indicate such a modification by colouring the evidence traceability link in red.

Text1: The system shall support requirements for codified data, including diagnosis data using ICD-9 codes.
Text2: The system shall include non-Standard Laboratory and Medication orderable files.

Text1: The system shall require a smart card reader, smart card, and a PIN to digitally sign an order.
Text2: The system shall require only a smart card to digitally sign an order.

Text1: The system shall allow medication orders to be placed and filled from local stocks of medications while providing full auditing of all medication orders within the medication profile.
Text2: The system shall allow medication orders to be placed and filled from local stocks of medications while providing auditing of most medication orders within the medication profile.

Text1: The system shall allow creation of personalized templates to fit the specific needs of individual practices.
Text2: The system shall allow creation of semi-personalized templates to fit the specific needs of individual practices.

Text1: The system shall allow medication orders to be placed and filled from local stocks of medications while providing full auditing of all medication orders within the medication profile.
Text2: The system shall mimic traditional order entry processes where necessary to accommodate established clinical practices.

Text1: The system shall check ICD and CPT code validity as of a specified date when codes are entered, when a new code set is implemented, and whenever CACs or IRM personnel choose to run the option.
Text2: The system shall only check ICD code validity as of a specified date when codes are entered, when a new code set is implemented, and whenever CACs or IRM personnel choose to run the option.

Text1: The system shall require most orders or documents, such as progress notes, reports, or health summaries, to have an electronic signature.
Text2: The system shall provide a means to view patient demographics, allergies and adverse reactions, postings (cautions, warnings, allergies, and advance directives), clinical reminders, current medications, problems, appointments, etc.

Text1: The system shall allow infinite hierarchies of subclasses to be created in the User Hierarchy Class structure.
Text2: The system shall allow 100 hierarchies of subclasses to be created in the User Hierarchy Class structure.

Text1: The system shall provide a method for identifying who is AUTHORIZED to do something (for example, sign and order).
Text2: Problems entered on the encounter form by clerical personnel shall be left as UNVERIFIED.

Text1: The system shall support audit capability.
Text2: The system shall include non-Standard Laboratory and Medication orderable files.

Text1: The system shall provide a way to limit the users or sites that have access to remote retrievable data.
Text2: The system shall select the item at the top of the list if the user does not enter enough characters to uniquely identify an item.

Text1: The system shall allow physician offices to use social security numbers or medical record numbers to identify patients.
Text2: The system shall allow physician offices to only use social security numbers to identify patients.

Text1: The system shall view users who hold the OREMAS key to be a clerical user, and, consequently, the verify, remove, restore, and view removed options will not be available.
Text2: The system shall provide tools for creating business rules that apply to documents used by members of such groups.

Text1: The system shall update diagnostic and procedure codes used for billing purposes so codes are applicable at the time the service was provided.
Text2: The system shall design the Kernel Person Class File to accommodate the HCFA National Provider System Taxonomy of Professionals/Occupations.

Text1: The system shall require handwritten signatures for outpatient medication orders of schedule 2 and schedule 2n controlled substances due to DEA policy.
Text2: The system shall require digital signatures for outpatient medication orders of schedule 2 and schedule 2n controlled substances due to DEA policy.

Text1: The system shall have pre-defined progress note templates.
Text2: The system shall integrate orders with progress notes, results, procedures, diagnosis, and Problem List.

Text1: The system shall delete history of received immunizations.
Text2: The system shall only delete history of recieved immunizations unless user specifies

Text1: The system shall indicate that a record is sensitive and may only be viewed by authorized users only if the user is not authorized.
Text2: The system shall support the Ambulatory Care Date Capture project for capturing encounter data for workload credit, as well as capturing clinically relevant data for reminders and reports.

Text1: The system shall allow medication orders to be placed and filled from local stocks of medications while providing auditing of most medication orders within the medication profile.
Text2: The system shall sort notifications based on column heading: Location (patient location, if known, alphabetical or reverse alphabetical).

Text1: The system's design shall follow hospital work flow patterns and allow local site and individual customization to make it fit specific needs.
Text2: The system's design shall follow hospital work flow patterns while minimizing individual customization in order to make the system similar across practises.

Text1: The system shall retain notifications for a predetermined amount of time (up to 30 days), after which they may be sent to another destination, such as your MailMan surrogate or your supervisor.
Text2: The system shall retain notifications for a predetermined amount of time (more than 30 days), after which they may be sent to another destination, such as your MailMan surrogate or your supervisor.

Text1: The system will abort when the user tries to login if tabs are not assigned to the user in their NEW PERSON file or if the RESTRICT PATIENT SELECTION prompt does not contain a Yes or No.
Text2: The system shall improve accessibility of online clinical information and results.

Text1: The system shall ensure that all team members receive necessary information about a patient by using patient lists to tie notifications to teams.
Text2: The system shall ensure that some team members receive all information about a patient by using patient lists to tie notifications to teams.

Text1: The system shall require the user to have access to the Clinical Coorindator menu in the List Manager Interface to assign CPRS tab access.
Text2: The system shall require the user to have access to the List Manager Interface to assign CPRS tab access.

Text1: Problems entered on the encounter form by clerical personnel shall be left as UNVERIFIED.
Text2: Problems entered on the encounter form by clerical personnel shall be left as UNSIGNED.

Text1: The system shall mimic traditional order entry processes where necessary to accommodate established clinical practices.
Text2: The system shall make the traditional order entry processes more practical.

Text1: The system shall only update diagnostic used for billing purposes so codes are applicable at the time the service was provided.
Text2: The system shall display a message that a patient has data from the legacy system used before the site was consolidated that is not being displayed which a user may want to access.

Text1: The system shall require the entry of an access code and a verify code to gain access to the system.
Text2: The system shall require the entry of an access code to gain access to the system.

Text1: The system shall design the Kernel Person Class File to accommodate the HCFA National Provider System Taxonomy of Professionals/Occupations.
Text2: The system shall include Standard Laboratory and Medication orderable files.

Text1: The system shall store which column the user sorted by when the user exits or changes patients and sorts by that column again when the Patient Selection/Notifications screen is next displayed.
Text2: The system shall not allow user classes to be used across VistaA to replace and/or complement security keys.

Text1: The Pilot controller shall be able to design, upload, start, follow, and disengage a flight path to the Hummingbird within the distance and direction to the Hummingbird.
Text2: The minimum time to fly a round trip of 40 miles shall be no more than 60 minutes under ideal conditions.

Text1: The Pilot shall be able to communicate with the custom communications network  within a 50-mile radius of the pilot station.
Text2: The Pilot shall be able to communicate with the UAV  within a 50-mile radius of the pilot station.

Text1: The Pilot controller shall detect instances of communication loss and automatically reconnect, if possible.
Text2: TheAviary is a system of systems consisting of a pilot controller, UAV , and <= 4 Viewers.

Text1: The UAV shall only accept commands from an authenticated Pilot controller.
Text2: The UAV shall be able to fly above 700 feet.

Text2: The UAV shall communicate with the Pilot Controller and a maximum of at least 6 Viewers.

Text1: The Pilot can communicate with the UAV using a custom communications network that can establish communication within a 50-mile radius of the pilot station.
Text2: The Pilot shall communicate with the UAV using custom communications network

Text1: The Pilot controller shall be able to command the Hummingbird to return to its starting position automously.
Text2: The UAV Viewer (also known as the "Viewer") shall securely connect to the UAV.

Text1: The Pilot controller shall automatically reconnect in the event of communication loss and automatically reconnect, if possible.
Text2: The Pilot controller shall detect instances of communication loss and manually reconnect, if possible.

Text1: The Pilot controller shall be able to call the Hummingbird to come back to its starting position autonomously.
Text2: The Pilot controller cannot request the Hummingbird to return to its starting position autonomously.

Text1: The Pilot controller shall initiate autonomous return of the Hummingbird to its starting position.
Text2: The Pilot controller shall be able to request the Hummungbird to return to its ending position autonomously.

Text1: The Pilot shall have near real-time visual feedback from the UAV's camera usable for the purpose of navigation and/or surveillance.
Text2: The Viewer shall connect with locally-available wireless networks.

Text1: The Viewer shall connect with the Aviary wireless network provided by the  SoS.
Text2: The Pilot shall be able to reset to default the zoom, aim and focus of the UAV camera in real-time.

Text1: The UAV shall be able to transmit video feed to the Pilot and up to 10 separate UAV Viewer devices.
Text2: The UAV shall be able to simultaneously transmit live video to the Pilot and no more than 4 UAV Viewer devices.

Text1: Using the Aviary SoS wireless network, the Viewer shall connect to the UAV.
Text2: The Viewer shall connect with locally-available wireless networks.

Text1: The UAV Viewer (also known as the "Viewer") shall connect to the UAV in an insecure fashion.
Text2: The UAV Viewer (also known as the "Viewer") shall connect to the UAV in an insecure fashion.

Text1: The Pilot shall be able to control the UAV camera in real-time.
Text2: The UAV Viewer (also known as the "Viewer") shall connect to the UAV using air waves.

Text1: The UAV shall be able to simultaneously transmit live video to the Pilot and no more than 4 UAV Viewer devices.
Text2: The aircraft shall be known as the Hummingbird.

Text1: Remote surveillance shall include video streaming for automatic navigation of the surveillance platform and for remote viewer by at least 8 independent operations personnel.
Text2: Remote surveillance shall include video streaming for manual navigation of the surveillance platform and for remote viewer by no less than 4 independent operations personnel.

Text1: The UAV shall be able to return to its starting position autonomously upon receiving a command from the Pilot or when a definable event has occurred.
Text2: The UAV shall be able to autonomously return to its starting position when a definable event has occurred or when receiving a command from the pilot.

Text1: The UAV shall maintain roll, pitch and yaw (attitude) in the presence of low to moderate winds.
Text2: The UAV visual feed shall have a night time heat sensing camera capability

Text1: The UAB shall periodically send the Pilot the power and estimated flight time remaining.
Text2: The UAB shall send the Pilot the power and estimated flight time remaining upon request.

Text1: The Pilot shall be able to control the speed of flight from 0 to up to 10 miles per hour in all directions while remaining horozontile to the plane.
Text2: The Pilot shall have total speed control over the speed of flight from 0 to maximum speed in any direction in a smooth fashion.

Text1: The UAV shall be able to fly above an above average 70 story building.
Text2: The UAV visual feed shall be free of motion artifacts for fast and moderate speed elements in its visual field.

Text1: The UAV shall support a stealth mode in which it emits no light during flight, which may be enabled or disabled from the Pilot control.
Text2: The UAV shall support a stealth mode in which it emits no sound during flight, which may be enabled or disabled from the Pilot control.

Text1: The Pilot shall rotate the UAV when it is moving north.
Text2: The UAV shall be visible to observers to at least 150m in daylight and 37m at night.

Text1: The UAV shall maintain attitude (roll, pitch, and yaw) in the presence of low to moderate winds.
Text2: The UAV shall maintain roll, pitch and yaw (attitude) in the presence of low to moderate winds.

Text1: The UAV's direction of flight shall be independent of its pitch (horizontal rotational orientation).
Text2: The UAV shall fully charge in less than 3 days.

Text1: The UAV shall save information about malfunctions that impact the mission.
Text2: The UAV shall send time-delayed altitude-above-floor information to the Pilot to facilitate landing, take off and object avoidance.

Text1: The UAV shall automatically land when communication with the pilot is lost and the remaining flight time is low.
Text2: The UAV shall be able to fly above an average 70 story building

Text1: The Pilot shall be able to fly the UAV to any accessible-by-air location within 32 kilometers of its origin
Text2: Communication established between flight, operations elements, and platform shall be secure for hacking and eavesdropping.

Text1: Communication among the flight, operations elements, and platform shall be secure for hacking and eavesdropping.
Text2: The Pilot shall rotate the UAV based on its direction of travel.

Text1: All UAV data transmission shall be secure against unauthorized access.
Text2: Only some UAV data transmission shall be secure against unauthorized access.

Text1: Communication established between flight, operations elements, and platform shall be secure for hacking and eavesdropping.
Text2: Communication along the flight and platform should be secure from hacking and eavesdropping.

Text1: The UAV shall be able to automatically a flight plan made up of a set of waypoints inside its range and flight capabilities.
Text2: The UAV shall detect instances of remote viewing app communication success and hover in position when communication is unstable.

Text1: Communication among the flight, operations elements, and platform shall not be secure for hacking and eavesdropping.
Text2: Communication among the flight, operations elements, and platform shall be secure for hacking and eavesdropping.

Text1: The custom communications network shall provide connections for access between the pilot and the UAV
Text2: The pilot's communication with the UAV shall require a secure connection.

Text1: The UAV shall detect instances of Pilot communication loss and both wait for communication to resume and hover in position until communication is reestablished.
Text2: The Pilot shall be able to observe the zoom, aim and focus of the UAV camera in real-time

Text1: The UAV shall land automatically when Pilot communication is lost and the estimated flight time remaining is less than 5 minutes.
Text2: The UAV shall automatically land when communication with Pilot is lost and estimated remaining flight time is less than 5 minutes.
